steps.title: Identify the contrast signal and what it requires
steps.content: The word "but" signals a contrast: Greek and Roman sculpture is "sometimes characterized as idealizing" subjects, but many sculptures depict people in "decidedly ______ ways." Idealizing means presenting subjects as perfect or beautiful. The blank must mean the opposite — something that describes people in an unfavorable or non-idealized way.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through all four choices:
  • Choice A — unflattering: "Unflattering" means presenting someone in an unattractive or unfavorable way — directly contrasting "idealizing," which presents subjects as perfect. This is the exact opposite the "but" requires. ✓
  • Choice B — superficial: "Superficial" means lacking depth. This contrasts thoroughness or depth of treatment, not beauty or idealization. A superficial sculpture could still depict someone flatteringly. ✗
  • Choice C — imprecise: "Imprecise" means inaccurate or vague. This contrasts accuracy, not idealization. An imprecise depiction could still idealize the subject. ✗
  • Choice D — implausible: "Implausible" means unbelievable or unrealistic. This contrasts believability, not idealization. An implausible depiction could still present the subject as beautiful and perfect. ✗
That matches Choice A — unflattering.
